channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: A Forza Horizon 6 Player Has Allegedly Been Banned For Nearly 8,000 Years
item.content:encoded:
The Forza Horizon 6 starting line is in sight, but a false start occurred, thanks to Playground Games itself. As a result, many have been able to play the game, and some are modding it. Doing so could land you a wild ban.

To catch you up, a Forza Horizon 6 leak reportedly dropped 155 GB of content online.

This has allowed many players to play the game early. However, if you’re found guilty of modding Forza Horizon 6, a big banhammer is reportedly coming your way.

Forza Horizon 6 Banning Players For Modded Copies

As shared by TheGameVerse, an early Forza Horizon 6 player has been banned until December 31, 9999!

Now, it’s fair to say that unless the ban is overturned, they’ll have to find a new way to play Forza Horizon 6 once the game is out. Unless human medicine evolves quickly, it’s unlikely the player will be around to see the end of the ban.

The incredible ban message appeared for the player (and possibly many others, according to reports), explaining they were ‘Suspended for Cheating/Unallowed Modding.’

The user allegedly tried to mod the game and implement cheats, and they were met with a slightly excessive ban. It’s not 100% confirmed that this is real, so take this with a pinch of salt.

But if they have tried to mod an unreleased game, there’s a good chance this message is real, and should serve as a strong warning to anyone else who considers doing the same.

Does the punishment fit the crime? Will a new day be on the horizon for the banned player? Share your thoughts on this fascinating story via the Insider Gaming Discord!

Check out other hot stories, including the Uncharted 4 documentary reveals huge gameplay and story differences in Amy Hennig’s version, and a new Xbox box art with ‘Grey Labels’ seemingly appears online.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: New Xbox Box Art with ‘Grey Labels’ Seemingly Appears Online
item.content:encoded:
Images of the new disc box art for Xbox consoles have surfaced online, with grey labels spotted on the boxes.

Since the arrival of the new CEO, we have been seeing ongoing changes to the Xbox brand. We have already seen the CEO bring a brand new logo and a boot-up sound to the table. Now, it seems they will be moving toward a brand-new style for their box art, which some players aren’t happy about.

New Xbox Box Art Spotted for Fortnite, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, and More Games

A new post on X (formerly Twitter) by Idle Sloth reveals the Xbox box art images that have appeared online, featuring games such as Alien Rogue Incursion, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, and Fortnite. The highlight of this new box art is the grey labels at the bottom of the game posters, which read “Game Disc” and “Requires Internet”. The Black Flag Resynced cover mentions that the internet is required to download content multiple times.

The grey bar appears to extend from the side of the disc box, where the game name is printed. Many players in the comment section have expressed their disappointment with this new design, raising concerns about the additional clutter near the art. There’s also an image of Beyond Good & Evil’s 20th Anniversary Edition disc, and the previous version looks much cleaner without the new grey labels. It is worth noting that Xbox has yet to confirm the new design officially, so take this information with a grain of salt.

In other news, there was no new information revealed about Project Helix at Xbox’s Game Dev Update event. Additionally, the company is no longer developing Co-Pilot for consoles. What are your thoughts on the new Xbox box art? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about changes towards Uncharted 4 gameplay and story being revealed in a new documentary. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Incredible Uncharted 4 Documentary Reveals Huge Gameplay & Story Differences in Amy Hennig Version
item.content:encoded:
The Uncharted 4 we got is one of the best games of all time and is, quite frankly, incredible. However, we very nearly didn’t get this version of Uncharted 4, and now the biggest drop of details ever has been revealed about Amy Hennig’s version of the game through an eye-opening documentary.

I brought to you the big story that Naughty Dog almost shut down Uncharted 4 because it ‘sucked.’ It was a startling revelation, and it’s amazing to think we nearly got a different game, or no game at all!

Get ready for the biggest deep dive on the ‘other’ Uncharted 4 yet.

Amy Hennig’s Uncharted 4 Was Quite Different

First of all, huge credit needs to go to Thekempy for the video. The 1:27:50-minute-long Uncharted 4 documentary chronicles everything about Uncharted 4—pre and post-Hennig’s influence.

They asked a simple question: ‘What was Amy Hennig’s version of Uncharted 4?’

Now, I’m obviously not going to go into everything, as this would be one of the longest articles in existence, but throughout the duration of the video, we see a ton of behind-the-scenes development on the game, mo-capping, cut content, and so much more.

But here are some fun details about Amy Hennig’s Uncharted 4:

  • There are many levels that never made it into the game.
  • Alternate story beats
  • Rafe was going to be played by Alan Tudyk, not Warren Kole.
  • Sam Drake was originally going to be played by Todd Stashwick, not Troy Baker.
  • Also, Sam was going to be more of a ‘villain’ character, although he still displayed some of those traits in the full game.
  • Uncharted 3’s cockey Charlie Cutter was supposed to return in Uncharted 4, but the revised game scrapped this.
  • The cut content included new gameplay mechanics.
  • There was no playable orphanage section in the game (is that a good or bad thing?)
  • 5:50 in shows early development footage of ‘Reef,’ where Nathan Drake isn’t doing normal work for a company in a regular day job, you’re hunting, with Elena and Sully, underwater, looking for the wreck of Henry Avery’s ship.

If you have the time, I encourage you to watch the whole thing. Even if you’re not interested in Uncharted 4 or the franchise in general, it’s an absorbing watch and shows how different Uncharted 4 could have been.

Here’s the full ‘Uncovering Amy Hennig’s Uncharted 4’ video.

Let us know if you ended up watching the whole thing, and what you learned, and head on over to the Insider Gaming Discord to share your favorite changes from the OG version of Uncharted 4.

For more news, read about LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight being fully playable well before launch, and the same happening to Forza Horizon 6.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Fnatic to Miss Valorant Masters London After Nightmare Playoffs
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic’s roster will miss the Valorant Masters London following their VCT EMEA Stage 1 playoffs exit at the hands of Team Heretics.

The result denies Fnatic and Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett the chance to play in front of a home crowd at London’s Copperbox Arena,

The classic series puts Fnatic in uncharted waters, missing their second international of the year for the first time in the organization’s history. Worst of all, it narrows their path to Valorant Champions 2026.

Team Heretics come up clutch against Fnatic

Following Team Vitality’s historic victory against Fnatic in the Upper Bracket, VCT EMEA’s most-celebrated roster had only one life left in the playoff bracket, requiring a clean run.

However, Team Heretics conjured some of their best form, combined with individual brilliance from Benjamin ‘benjyfishy’ Fish and series MVP Enes ‘RieNs’ Ecirli, to take the series 2-1, eliminating Fnatic from London contention.

The series was defined by clutch scenarios, especially on map 3 of Ascent. On Fnatic’s side, Emir ‘Alfajer’ Beder time and time again was keeping Fnatic in the series and in Masters London contention.

At 12-9 up, it seemed that Alfajer would essentially drag Fnatic past Team Heretics singlehandedly, but with three map and match points, Fnatic’s demons of EMEA Kickoff began to haunt them again.

Then, Gentle Mates had denied them a place at Masters Santiago from a 12-11 lead, after that Liquid fought back from 12-10 to deny Fnatic Abyss in the Lower Final.

Now, at 12-9, Fnatic got tight while Team Heretics continued to be at their expressive best, as they fought back to claim overtime.

While RieNs fought to put Heretics in that position, it was third-clutch lucky for Ričardas ‘Boo’ Lukaševičius as the IGL delivered in a 1v2 clutch, with only two bullets left in his mag to close out the series 15-13 and eliminate Fnatic.

While it is a series that will be remembered for both individual brilliance and individual mistakes, what Fnatic truly suffered from in the VCT EMEA playoffs was beyond the individual. It was the price of a disrupted season.

Veqaj expresses guilt over Fnatic exit

Ultimately, what has underlined Fnatic for so many years in Valorant is their incredible teamplay, protocols, and control of maps and situations. Against Team Heretics, the series became a total brawl, with no control to speak of on either side.

For Team Heretics, chaos is where they have always thrived, but for Fnatic the reason for their lack of control is clear.

Sylvain ‘Veqaj’ Pattyn’s absence due to health issues interrupted Fnatic’s up til then flawless season. The player was then officially benched, with Clément ‘CyvOph’ Millard joining for the last group stage game of the season and the playoffs.

There is no question of moral culpability, as health should always come first, but for Fnatic the timing was extremely unfortunate.

CyvOph helped Fnatic to deliver a 5-0 Group Stage record, but playoff Valorant is a different beast, and with so little time to work out protocols, positions, and player synergy, Fnatic looked disconnected against Team Heretics.

The most bitter pill to swallow will be how good Fnatic were looking with Veqaj prior to his unfortunate health issues. The Frenchman had become a selfless lynchpin of Fnatic’s game plan, with key utility usage and selfless aggro plays setting up Fnatic’s stars for success.

Veqaj shared on social media, “I’m really sad about tonight. They truly deserved to go to London, and I can’t help but feel a little guilty too.” Later adding, “Please send lots of love to the boys. It’s heartbreaking, but I know we’ll get through this.”

The player also gave an update on his condition, stating, “As for how I’m feeling, you can count on me to be ready as soon as possible.”

Fnatic’s Valorant Champions 2026 position on the line

And Fnatic will need Veqaj sooner rather than later. VCT EMEA Stage 2 will be all or nothing for the org, as their place at Valorant Champions is now under severe threat.

Despite the five VCT Points from group stage wins, it will be difficult for Fnatic to qualify through points alone, as it will effectively be out of their hands.

It would take VCT EMEA teams to perform poorly at Valorant Masters London, and for the teams already at the top of the VCT EMEA Points standings to not do well in Stage 2, for Fnatic to get a position purely through VCT Points.

Additionally, there are still 15 points to be distributed amongst the VCT EMEA Stage 1 playoffs top four, making Fnatic’s path narrower.

The path for Fnatic to Valorant Champions Shanghai may instead be through winning or coming in as runners-up in VCT EMEA Stage 2, requiring elite performance in a disrupted season.

Do you think Fnatic will make it to Shanghai? Let us know on our Discord!
For more Valorant news, see Eggsterr’s departure from pro play.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight Has Been Fully Playable For Some
item.content:encoded:
Everyone’s games are being released or made playable early for some reason in May 2026. Another huge game leak has made it so that the much-anticipated LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight is fully playable for select users.

Just when you thought May couldn’t get any more bizarre, it does. A huge Forza Horizon 6 leak reportedly dropped 155 GB of content online. Simply, Forza Horizon 6 was playable ahead of its launch date. A similar thing has seen the Caped Crusader make the lives of Gotham scum hell ahead of time in LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight.

LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight Released Early on Xbox

Reddit user Dramatic-Track-9935 shared many pictures showing them with a fully installed LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight.

The evidence looks very real, and some have indeed been playing LEGO Batman: Legacy of The Dark Knight early.

It seems the game was only playable via a ‘Walmart digital code.’ If you pre-ordered the game this way and started the preload, you got immediate access to the game once the download was complete.

Unfortunately, if you’re hoping to do this yourself, you’re going to be out of luck, as it seems this has now been patched out, locking out anyone who’s been able to play Batman early.

If you want some sneaky details about the game or see the game in action for yourself, check out the Reddit thread below.

Were you one of the lucky people who managed to play LEGO Batman: The Dark Knight first? Or are you on the outside looking inside at these fortunate few with envy and jealousy? I don’t blame you, but let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out ‘What’s Wrong With Mixtape?‘ for an interesting look at one of the games of the year.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: ARC Raiders Fourth Expedition Project Guide—Exact Expedition Window & Project Requirements
item.content:encoded:
The third Expedition Window has officially closed, and it’s already time to start looking ahead towards the next one. The Rust Belt may be filled with freshly wiped accounts, but it’s never too early to start looking ahead towards the upcoming Expedition.

Of course, you don’t have to reset, and the latest wipe had a lot of controversy surrounding the damage dealing during a certain timed window, but Embark Studios is actively working on improving the rewards and process, wipe by wipe. By the time the fourth Expedition Window opens, it might be a no-brainer to wipe your account, so you’ll want to make sure you’re prepared.

Completing the Project is no quick and easy feat, so it’s best to get a head start. With that in mind, here’s when the fourth Expedition Window will open in ARC Raiders, and what you’ll need to complete the Project.

When Is ARC Raiders’ Fourth Expedition Window?

The fourth Expedition Window will open on June 22, 2026, meaning you’ve got practically a month and a half since the Project opened to complete it.

That’s almost two months from when the Project first becomes available, and if you didn’t complete the previous Expedition, you’ll still have whatever progress you made towards it saved from before. You can find the live countdown when you preview the Project in-game, so you can always stay up to date.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Destroy The Turbine In ARC Raiders—Turbine Compressor, Explained

The end date of the wipe is yet to be confirmed, and given the reactive nature of the last Expedition, it could be anywhere from five days after the Window opens to a whole two weeks later. It largely depends on whether Embark insist on damage dealt being the indicator to earn extra Skill Points, but we’ll have to wait until June to see how it’s handled.

Still, it might seem like that’s a lot of time to complete the Expedition Project, but be warned, you’ll need a lot of different resources if you want to finish it in time. It’s best to start sooner rather than later, as you might wind up missing the wipe and regretting it.

What Are The Requirements For The Fourth Expedition Project In ARC Raiders?

There are five stages to the Expedition Project in ARC Raiders, with each one having four requirements.

  • More Insider Gaming: Beachcombing Map Condition In ARC Raiders—Where To Find Dockmaster’s Detector

The items vary from materials to ARC parts, but the toughest stage is undoubtedly the final one, which requires you to sell 750,000 Coins’ worth of supplies! The Expedition won’t come cheap, but if you want to start completing it, here are all of the Project’s stages:

Will you be completing the fourth Expedition for ARC Raiders? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more ARC Raiders content, check out the following:

  • ARC Raiders Datamine Uncovers New Map, Trading, Events, and More
  • ARC Raiders Review, But Six Months Later

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
